_comment: sovereign-os Auditor dashboard (R497, master spec §§ 10, 17). Visualizes the operator-§17 Immutable Gatekeeper — the always-on, kernel-driven, podman-kill-armed eBPF event-loop guardian. Surfaces per-result neutralization counts, per-outcome event-parse classification, and last-neutralization freshness gauge. Operator imports into Grafana via Settings → JSON Model. Closes the auditor dashboard:FUTURE waiver and registers `auditor` as a first-class MODULE_COVERAGE entry (4 shipped surfaces: core / cli / service / dashboard).

panels.title: neutralizations (total)
panels.description: Sum-since-start of perimeter-violation neutralizations. Operator-§10 podman-kill outcomes across all results.

panels.title: neutralizations (success)
panels.description: Operator-§10 happy-path: result="success" — podman kill returned cleanly + audit log appended.

panels.title: last violation age (s)
panels.description: Operator-§10 freshness signal — staleness of the LAST neutralization. Green = no recent violations (quiet perimeter); transitioning to non-green = active threat just neutralized.

panels.title: events parsed (total)
panels.description: Sum-since-start across all 3 parse outcomes (trigger / benign / bad-json). Operator-§10 raw event-stream throughput off the Tetragon eBPF UNIX socket.

panels.title: neutralization rate per result (5m)
panels.description: Per-result rate. Result vocabulary: success / kill-failed / no-container-id / dry-run / *+audit-log-fail. Operator alerts on kill-failed or audit-log-fail rates > 0.

panels.title: event-parse rate per outcome (5m)
panels.description: Per-outcome event-parse rate. trigger = perimeter violation matched master spec § 10.1 predicate; benign = parsed but not a trigger; bad-json = unparseable line off the Tetragon stream.

panels.title: cumulative neutralizations per result
panels.description: Sum-since-start per result. Operator-§10 lifetime outcome distribution. Tall success bar = healthy circuit breaker. Tall kill-failed bar = podman runtime issue.

panels.title: cumulative parse outcomes
panels.description: Sum-since-start per outcome. Operator-§10 event-stream distribution. Heavy benign = quiet perimeter; heavy trigger = active attack surface.

panels.title: auditor decision matrix (outcome × cumulative count)
panels.description: Cross-tab of event-parse outcomes since metrics start. Operator-discoverable: 'what fraction of the Tetragon stream is malformed vs benign vs triggering?'

options.content:
**Master spec § 10 verbatim** (Native Guardian Event Loop, sacrosanct):

> "To replace the legacy Windows-centric SecureToast.ps1 concept without introducing visual or network bloat, we introduce a lightweight, native Linux event supervisor. This daemon listens to the local Tetragon eBPF UNIX socket and acts as an autonomous circuit breaker."

**Master spec § 17 — The Auditor (Immutable Gatekeeper):**

The Auditor is one of the three §17 Genesis Trinity members:

1. **The Weaver** — atomic state-transition primitive (master spec § 21, R496-dashboarded)
2. **The Auditor** — always-on, kernel-driven, podman-kill-armed event-loop guardian (master spec § 10, R497-dashboarded — THIS dashboard)
3. **The Architect** — operator-§17 system architect role

**Operator §10 circuit-breaker protocol (master spec § 10.1 verbatim hot path):**

1. Tail `/var/run/tetragon/tetragon.events` (kernel eBPF event stream)
2. Parse each JSON line
3. Trigger predicate (verbatim): `event.get('action') == 'SIGKILL' or 'process' in event.get('action', '').lower()`
4. On trigger: `podman kill <container_id>` (5s timeout)
5. Append violation line to `/mnt/vault/context/security_audit.log` (master spec § 7.1 atomic append-only path; tank/context sync=always per § 7.2)
6. Emit Layer-B counter per neutralization

**3 parse-outcome vocabulary (Layer-B `event_parse_total{outcome}`):**

1. `trigger` — event matched the § 10.1 predicate; neutralization fired
2. `benign` — event parsed cleanly but didn't match the predicate
3. `bad-json` — Tetragon emitted an unparseable line (operator alerts if rate > 0)

**4+ neutralization-result vocabulary (Layer-B `neutralization_total{result}`):**

1. `success` — `podman kill` returned 0 + audit log appended
2. `kill-failed` — `podman kill` raised OSError/TimeoutExpired
3. `no-container-id` — event had no `process.docker` field (master spec § 10.1 field extraction)
4. `dry-run` — GUARDIAN_DRY_RUN=1 set (parse-only mode)
5. Compound: `<result>+audit-log-fail` — neutralized but audit-log append failed

**Layer-B metric contract (R497-dashboarded):**

1. Metric: `sovereign_os_auditor_neutralization_total{result}` (counter)
2. Metric: `sovereign_os_auditor_event_parse_total{outcome}` (counter)
3. Metric: `sovereign_os_auditor_last_neutralization_timestamp` (freshness gauge — operator alerts on any non-stale reading)

**Service:**

```
systemctl status  sovereign-guardian-core.service    # daemon liveness
systemctl restart sovereign-guardian-core.service    # operator-controlled restart
journalctl -u     sovereign-guardian-core.service -f # live event-loop tail
```

Unit lives at `systemd/system/sovereign-guardian-core.service` and declares `After=tetragon.service Requires=tetragon.service` (master spec § 10.2 verbatim ordering).

**Env vars (all overridable, operator-§10 sacrosanct defaults):**

- `GUARDIAN_SOCKET_PATH` — default `/var/run/tetragon/tetragon.events`
- `GUARDIAN_AUDIT_LOG` — default `/mnt/vault/context/security_audit.log`
- `GUARDIAN_PODMAN_BIN` — default `podman`
- `GUARDIAN_DRY_RUN` — set to `1` to parse-only (no kill, no log write)

**Source:** master spec §§ 10 and 17 shipped the Auditor blueprint; R152-R155 shipped the Python implementation + systemd unit + Layer-B metrics; R497 ships THIS dashboard + registers `auditor` as a first-class MODULE_COVERAGE entry (4 surfaces: core / cli / service / dashboard).

**Standing operator rule (sacrosanct):**

> "We do not minimize anything."

title: sovereign-os auditor
